plan_adherence_notes: All 6 plan steps were executed faithfully. Step 1 scaffolding (mocks, fixtures, helpers, Seeder pattern) matches decision d1 exactly. Steps 2-5 produced 19 tests covering all specified behaviors. Step 6 confirmed all tests pass. Minor deviation: plan step 3 mentioned verifying 'ASSUMED: outcomeId' badge via seeded assumptions, but the implementation tests it via click interaction instead — functionally equivalent and arguably better since it tests the full add/remove flow through real context. The conditional variableId test (d2) correctly verifies both code paths (isTargetMarket true/false).
missed_questions: How does AssumptionContext key assumptions — by variableId or marketId? (The hasAssumption call uses m.id but addAssumption stores variableId; the test fixture had to set variableId === id to make the toggle work, which is a subtle coupling that could have been explored.)
Should the test verify contextPayload contents (not just toBeDefined) when assumptions are seeded before an edit submission?
reason: All 19 tests pass and cover the 4 behavioral areas from the task description (rendering, assumption toggle, edit flow, mutation feedback). The plan was well-researched and faithfully executed. Mock strategy follows codebase conventions (TradingPanel pattern for hooks, AssumptionPanel Seeder pattern for context). The conditional variableId logic — the most important branch in VariableRow — is explicitly tested with both code paths. The two missed questions are minor: the variableId/marketId keying issue was handled correctly in practice, and contextPayload contents are a nice-to-have assertion. No production code was modified.
